description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinctive, industrial look. The stone appears to be a dark, speckled material, likely granite or a similar dense rock, with a rough, matte finish. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, emphasizing its mass and durability. The grooves are consistent in width and depth, suggesting the block is designed for drainage, traction, or as a visual deterrent. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier or obstacle to control pedestrian or vehicular movement.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across its top surface, giving it a striped, industrial appearance. The block is solid and heavy, likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the grooves again visible running horizontally across the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with vertical grooves and a clear view of the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or drainage block
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with pavement or other blocks in a continuous row
